bash_commands.command: find /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission -type f -name "*.go" | grep -v test | grep -v _test | sort
find /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0 -type f -path "*/admission/plugin/webhook/*" -name "*.go" | grep -v test | grep -v _test | wc -l

cd /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0 && for file in \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/plugin.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go" \
  "pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/config.go"; do
  if [ -f "$file" ]; then
    echo "✓ $file"
  else
    echo "✗ $file NOT FOUND"
  fi
done

for file in \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/interfaces.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/configuration_manager.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/namespace/matcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/object/matcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/matchconditions/matcher.go" \
  "pkg/controlplane/apiserver/admission/config.go"; do
  if [ -f "$file" ]; then
    echo "✓ $file"
  else
    echo "✗ $file NOT FOUND"
  fi
done

  "text": "The Kubernetes API server admission webhook chain consists of multiple components located in the staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver module. The core admission framework is defined in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/, which provides the chainAdmissionHandler (chain.go) that chains multiple admission handlers together. The chain implements the Admit() and Validate() methods to sequentially process requests through admission handlers, returning immediately on the first error.\n\nThe webhook admission controller is implemented as a generic Webhook class (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go) that orchestrates webhook calls. This generic webhook is used by two concrete plugins: MutatingAdmissionWebhook (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go) and ValidatingAdmissionWebhook (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/plugin.go).\n\nThe webhook dispatch logic is handled by separate dispatchers for mutating (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go) and validating (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go) webhooks. These dispatchers iterate through registered webhooks, determine if they should be called based on namespace selectors, object selectors, and rule matching, and then invoke the webhook via callAttrMutatingHook or similar methods.\n\nWebhook configurations are managed by configuration managers: mutatingWebhookConfigurationManager (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go) and validatingWebhookConfigurationManager (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go). These managers maintain cached views of dynamic webhook configurations from the API server.\n\nThe webhook call dispatch uses WebhookAccessor (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go) to provide a common interface to both mutating and validating webhook metadata. Request/response handling is performed in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go, which handles creation of AdmissionReview objects and verification of webhook responses.\n\nAdmission response aggregation is performed in the dispatchers, particularly in the mutatingDispatcher.Dispatch() method, which processes webhook responses, applies patches from mutating webhooks, and handles reinvocation context when webhooks modify the object.\n\nPredicate matching logic is split across multiple files: namespace selector matching (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/namespace/matcher.go), object selector matching (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/object/matcher.go), and rule matching (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/rules/rules.go). Match conditions are handled by staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/matchconditions/matcher.go.\n\nThe API server integration points are in pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/ and pkg/controlplane/apiserver/admission/, which initialize and configure admission plugins for the API server and control plane respectively."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Kubernetes API Server Admission Webhook Chain

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the API server admission webhook chain: the webhook admission controller, the webhook call dispatch, and the admission response aggregation.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the domain domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all the Go source files that implement the API server admission webhook chain in the Kubernetes repository. Let me start by exploring the codebase structure.
Now let me search for additional key files and then compile the complete answer.

Perfect! I have successfully identified and documented all the Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the API server admission webhook chain. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Summary

I found **44 Go source files** that implement the Kubernetes API server admission webhook chain, distributed across these key components:

### Core Architecture
1. **Admission Chain Framework** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/`)
   - `chain.go` - Chains multiple admission handlers sequentially
   - `interfaces.go` - Core admission plugin interfaces
   - `handler.go` - Handler base class

2. **Generic Webhook Infrastructure** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/`)
   - `generic/webhook.go` - Generic webhook orchestrator
   - `generic/interfaces.go` - Dispatcher and Source interfaces
   - `accessors.go` - WebhookAccessor for unified webhook access

3. **Plugin Implementations** 
   - Mutating webhook: `plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go` + `dispatcher.go`
   - Validating webhook: `plugin/webhook/validating/plugin.go` + `dispatcher.go`

4. **Configuration Management**
   - `configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go` - Manages mutating webhook configs
   - `configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go` - Manages validating webhook configs

5. **Request/Response Processing**
   - `plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go` - Creates and verifies AdmissionReview objects

6. **Predicate & Match Logic**
   - Namespace, object, and rule matching
   - CEL-based match condition evaluation

7. **API Server Integration**
   - `pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/` - kube-apiserver specific config
   - `pkg/controlplane/apiserver/admission/` - Generic API server integration

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed information about all files, key symbols, and the execution chain.

    {"repo": "kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go", "symbol": "VerifyAdmissionResponse"}
  ],
  "text": "The Kubernetes API server admission webhook chain consists of multiple components located in the staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver module. The core admission framework is defined in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/, which provides the chainAdmissionHandler (chain.go) that chains multiple admission handlers together. The chain implements the Admit() and Validate() methods to sequentially process requests through admission handlers, returning immediately on the first error.\n\nThe webhook admission controller is implemented as a generic Webhook class (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go) that orchestrates webhook calls. This generic webhook is used by two concrete plugins: MutatingAdmissionWebhook (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go) and ValidatingAdmissionWebhook (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/plugin.go).\n\nThe webhook dispatch logic is handled by separate dispatchers for mutating (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go) and validating (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go) webhooks. These dispatchers iterate through registered webhooks, determine if they should be called based on namespace selectors, object selectors, and rule matching, and then invoke the webhook via callAttrMutatingHook or similar methods.\n\nWebhook configurations are managed by configuration managers: mutatingWebhookConfigurationManager (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go) and validatingWebhookConfigurationManager (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go). These managers maintain cached views of dynamic webhook configurations from the API server.\n\nThe webhook call dispatch uses WebhookAccessor (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go) to provide a common interface to both mutating and validating webhook metadata. Request/response handling is performed in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go, which handles creation of AdmissionReview objects and verification of webhook responses.\n\nAdmission response aggregation is performed in the dispatchers, particularly in the mutatingDispatcher.Dispatch() method, which processes webhook responses, applies patches from mutating webhooks, and handles reinvocation context when webhooks modify the object.\n\nPredicate matching logic is split across multiple files: namespace selector matching (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/namespace/matcher.go), object selector matching (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/object/matcher.go), and rule matching (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/rules/rules.go). Match conditions are handled by staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/matchconditions/matcher.go.\n\nThe API server integration points are in pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/ and pkg/controlplane/apiserver/admission/, which initialize and configure admission plugins for the API server and control plane respectively."
}

arguments.command:
cd /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0 && for file in \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/plugin.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go" \
  "pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/config.go"; do
  if [ -f "$file" ]; then
    echo "✓ $file"
  else
    echo "✗ $file NOT FOUND"
  fi
done

for file in \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/interfaces.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/configuration_manager.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/namespace/matcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/predicates/object/matcher.go" \
  "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/matchconditions/matcher.go" \
  "pkg/controlplane/apiserver/admission/config.go"; do
  if [ -f "$file" ]; then
    echo "✓ $file"
  else
    echo "✗ $file NOT FOUND"
  fi
done
